title: Post-process with per-stint stop-count mass constraints
summary: ROC AUC depends on ranking, but raw probabilities may assign too much positive mass across many laps in the same stint.
rationale: ROC AUC depends on ranking, but raw probabilities may assign too much positive mass across many laps in the same stint. A constrained calibrator can preserve most within-stint order while matching plausible remaining stop counts implied by rules, tyre age, and historical stint patterns.
implementation_hint: After OOF predictions, fit a lightweight meta-calibrator using base logit, Race-Year, Stint, LapsRemaining, current stop count, and compound. Within each driver-race-stint, shrink or cap probability mass toward learned expected remaining stops while preserving rank as much as possible.
expected_effect: Improves cross-stint ordering and reduces overconfident repeated pit probabilities after a high-risk lap passes.
risk: Aggressive per-group normalization can hurt AUC by changing global ranks; compare sigmoid-only, isotonic, and mass-constrained variants on grouped folds.
